channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Government Registers Aliens.Gov Domain as Next Distraction From Everything Else
item.description: The truth is out there (on the internet).

item.title: Nothing Headphone A vs. Headphone 1: Which Pair of ANC Wireless Headphones Wins?
item.description: Nothing versus Nothing is really something.

item.title: ‘Witch Hat Atelier’ Is Simply Magical
item.description: Bug Films' anime take on Kamome Shirahama's beloved fantasy series, due on Crunchyroll this April, was well worth the wait.

item.title: After 80 hours of Crimson Desert I'm thirsty for more of its breathtaking open-world and enthralling set pieces — it just needs a few tweaks in some areas to become a masterpiece
item.description: Crimson Desert provides a fantastic open-world experience, which thrives in puzzle-solving, finding surprises, and combat that rivals the best out there.

item.title: The FBI is buying Americans’ location data
item.description: FBI director Kash Patel admitted that the agency is buying location data that can be used to track people's movements. Unlike information obtained from cell phone providers, this data can be accessed without a warrant - and used to track anyone. "We do purchase commercially available information that's consistent with the Constitution and the laws […]...

item.title: Kash Patel Admits the FBI is Buying Private Data on Americans
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden called it an "outrageous end run around the Fourth Amendment."

item.title: Apples new budget MacBook Neo is already on sale for the first time ever
item.description: The new Apple MacBook Neo is on sale on Amazon as of March 18. Save $4 on the base model and about $9 on the Touch ID version.

item.title: The PS5 Pro Is Now the Best, Priciest, and Most Piecemeal Console You Can Buy
item.description: We tested the console's new PSSR upscaler and found it enhances some games more than others.

item.title: Unbelievably thin Panther Lake mini PC pictured with 13 ports on all four sides, including a 10GbE LAN connector, an OCuLink port — and, wait for it, three M.2 slots inside
item.description: FEVM’s ultra-thin mini PC packs Panther Lake CPU, three M.2 slots, 10GbE, and dense ports into laptop-like dimensions.

item.title: Hackers target millions of iPhones with new DarkSword spyware
item.description: Cybersecurity groups are sounding the alarm about DarkSword, the latest hacker tool that targets iPhone users.

item.title: You Haven’t Seen the ‘Dune: Part Three’ Trailer Until You’ve Seen It in Franken-IMAX
item.description: A fan realized if you combine the Instagram and YouTube versions, you get IMAX. Kind of.

item.title: Can you get fired for calling your CEO a “rich jerk”? This company says yes
item.description: In a recent hearing, the National Labor Relations Board argued that software company Atlassian illegally fired an employee for criticizing the CEO. A new case in front of labor regulators could answer a question many workers might have contemplated. Can your employer fire you for speaking out against the CEO?

item.title: Polymarket is opening a bar where you can drink and watch the world unravel in real time
item.description: The company is turning its prediction platform into a physical hangout for traders and policy watchers. The nation’s capital is getting a new Situation Room this weekend. But rather than generals and high ranking officials, this D.C bar is for the people “monitoring the situation.”

item.title: Amazon doesn’t want the blame for the Post Office going under
item.description: Amazon is going on the defensive after a report from The Wall Street Journal revealed that the ecommerce giant plans to slash shipments with the United States Postal Service, which said this week that it's running out of money. In a lengthy statement published on Wednesday, Amazon says it didn't want to reduce shipments with […]

item.title: Americans Seem to Love Buying Cars That Get Hit With a Lot of Recalls
item.description: An analysis by a law firm specializing in car accidents ranked the cars with the most recalls that American consumers keep buying.

item.title: Congress considers blowing up internet law
item.description: Internet platforms' liability shield Section 230 faced another round of attack at a Senate Commerce Committee hearing on Wednesday, this time with two distinct undercurrents complicating the conversation. One was an unprecedented wave of ongoing legal challenges to the law's scope, and the second was a heightened bipartisan concern over government censorship. "Section 230 is […]...

item.title: Golf balls, ties and an RTX 5090: All the best things you can buy at the Nvidia GTC 2026 merch store
item.description: We investigated the Nvidia GTC 2026 merch store, so you don’t have to.

item.title: A new iPhone hacking tool puts anyone still on iOS 18 at risk
item.description: Google and cybersecurity companies Lookout and iVerify have detailed a new hacking technique that potentially puts a significant portion of iPhone users in danger, just by visiting the wrong web page. The hack is called "DarkSword" and since it specifically targets several different versions of iOS 18, it could affect "close to a quarter of iPhones," Wired writes.DarkSword is a "fileless" hack that leverages a collection of exploits to access sensitive data when an iPhone visits an infected website. Rather than install spyware that hangs around on a user's phone after messages and other privat...
